title: For a Hard Season of Parenting
scripture_text: As one whom his mother comforts, so will I comfort you.
reflection: You are not the only one who has parented a hard child. The Lord Himself has. The cross was His parenting moment.
body:
The hard year. The year of the door slamming. The year of the lying. The year of the kid you do not recognize. You have been doing this alone, and you are tired in a way that sleep does not fix.

This is not because you are failing. This is because parenting a hard child is hard. It is one of the hardest things a human being can do. It will not be this way forever.

The Lord is with you. He is the God of the door-slammers. He was a door-slammer Himself, at twelve, in the temple, worrying His mother half to death.

Take a breath. The kid is still a kid. The love is still the love. The hard year will end. You will come out the other side, scarred, wiser, and with a new respect for every parent in the world.
prayer: Lord, hold this child. And hold me. We are both going to make it. By Your grace. Amen.
